role_summary: Civil rights movement leader and educator; co-organizer of the 1961 Rock Hill, South Carolina Friendship 9 lunch-counter sit-in; later a SNCC field organizer and CORE staff member.
ai_reading: Gaither's top semantic neighbor is C. T. Vivian (entry 134, count 147), the SCLC direct-action teacher; his next neighbors are Charles F. McDew (entry 16, SNCC chairman 1961-63 who co-led the March 1960 Orangeburg protest at South Carolina State), Charles McLaurin (entry 17, Mississippi SNCC), Dion Diamond (entry 27, Howard NAG and Freedom Rider), and Joan Trumpauer Mulholland (entry 60, Freedom Rider). The cluster is the early-1960s direct-action cohort: SCLC (Vivian) and SNCC (McDew, Diamond, Mulholland) figures who shared the lunch-counter and Freedom-Rides organizing of 1960-1961. The pairing with McDew specifically (count 126) is direct biographical alignment: McDew led the Orangeburg sit-in, Gaither led the Rock Hill Friendship 9 sit-in, both in 1960. Gaither's axis positions are all moderate (range -0.12 to +0.46), placing him near the catalog's centrist voices.
biographical_paragraph: Thomas Walter Gaither's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 119 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Independent biographical sourcing is pending; the strongest signal currently available for movement role is the embedding pattern visible in the AI's reading above.

sources.title: Thomas Walter Gaither oral history
